text: Handling deadlines is a key skill in both work and study. Effective time management starts with breaking tasks into smaller steps and setting mini-deadlines. Prioritization is also important—ask yourself which tasks are urgent or have the biggest impact, and do them first. Sometimes a project feels overwhelming because its scope is too big. Scope management means focusing on what is essential and avoiding unnecessary additions. Finally, communication matters. If you cannot meet a deadline, tell your team or manager early. They may adjust the deadline or help you reprioritize. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and complete your work on time.
